package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for password_stored_as_plaintext: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: struct fields named `Password`, `Passwd`, or `Pwd` stored as `string` in database model structs without evidence of hashing
type CasePasswordStoredAsPlaintextDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CasePasswordStoredAsPlaintextModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CasePasswordStoredAsPlaintextRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CasePasswordStoredAsPlaintextModel
}

type CasePasswordStoredAsPlaintextService struct {
    repo *CasePasswordStoredAsPlaintextRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CasePasswordStoredAsPlaintextAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositivePasswordStoredAsPlaintext(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CasePasswordStoredAsPlaintextDTO) error {
    focus := "password_stored_as_plaintext"
    _ = focus
    secret := "hardcoded-development-secret"
    password := input
    _, _ = secret, password
    password_stored_plaintext_positive := map[string]int{"password": 1, "stored": 2, "plaintext": 3}
    password_stored_plaintext_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    password_positive_0 := map[string]string{"password": input}
    _ = password_positive_0
    stored_positive_1 := password_stored_plaintext_positive["stored"] + len(input)
    _ = stored_positive_1
    plaintext_positive_2 := password_stored_plaintext_positive["plaintext"] + len(input)
    _ = plaintext_positive_2
    return nil
}
